title: How a Leading Functional Medicine Doctor Balances Healing Others and Motherhood
guestBio: Dr. Mary "Sunny" Raleigh is a renowned functional medicine doctor and founder of Restoration Healthcare in Irvine, California. She specializes in treating complex chronic conditions like Lyme disease and autoimmune disorders that traditional medicine often overlooks, and is a mother of three teenage boys.
excerpt: Dr. Sunny Raleigh shares how she built a thriving functional medicine practice while raising three boys across multiple states. Plus, her groundbreaking insights on the gut-brain connection that could transform your health.
metaDescription: Functional medicine expert Dr. Sunny Raleigh reveals how to balance high-impact business with motherhood, plus powerful gut-brain healing insights.
keyTakeaways: The gut-brain connection plays a crucial role in healing chronic conditions and overall wellness.
Distance parenting and co-parenting can work when you're intentional about showing up fully for your children.
Small, consistent health changes create more impact than dramatic overhauls.
Supporting concussion recovery requires a holistic approach that addresses both physical and neurological healing.
You can run a successful medical practice while managing family responsibilities across multiple states.
Treating patients that traditional medicine overlooks requires both medical expertise and deep compassion.
Being a working mother means making tough choices but staying committed to both your mission and your family.

pullQuotes.text: I treat the patients no one else can—those suffering from conditions that traditional medicine often overlooks.

timestamps.label: Dr. Sunny's journey into functional medicine
Managing a clinic from Idaho while parenting three boys
The gut-brain connection and healing insights
Small changes for big health impact
Final thoughts and where to find Dr. Sunny

content:
What does it take to build a revolutionary medical practice while raising three teenage boys across multiple states? In this powerful conversation, I sit down with my dear friend Dr. Mary "Sunny" Raleigh, and the answer might surprise you with its blend of fierce determination and radical flexibility.

Dr. Sunny isn't your typical doctor. As the founder of Restoration Healthcare in Irvine, California, she's built her reputation on treating the patients that traditional medicine often gives up on. We're talking about people suffering from Lyme disease, autoimmune disorders, and chronic conditions that have left them feeling hopeless after countless doctor visits and dead-end diagnoses.

But here's what makes this conversation so compelling for us as women who lead both at home and at work: Dr. Sunny is doing all of this while managing her clinic from Idaho, commuting across states, and showing up fully for her three teenage boys, including one who's about to graduate high school. If you've ever felt like you're being pulled in multiple directions while trying to make an impact, this episode will both inspire and ground you.

The Reality of Distance Leadership

One of the most honest parts of our conversation centers around what it actually looks like to run a high-impact business while prioritizing your family. Dr. Sunny doesn't sugarcoat the challenges of distance parenting or pretend that managing a medical practice from another state is simple. Instead, she shares the practical realities of co-parenting and the intentional choices she makes to ensure she's present for the moments that matter most.

What struck me most was her approach to presence over proximity. While she can't be physically present for every moment, she's created systems and boundaries that allow her to show up fully when she is with her boys. This isn't about having it all—it's about being strategic with your energy and attention so you can excel in the areas that matter most to you.

Revolutionary Healing Through the Gut-Brain Connection

Beyond her inspiring approach to work-life integration, Dr. Sunny shares groundbreaking insights about health that could transform how you think about your own wellness journey. The gut-brain connection isn't just medical jargon—it's a powerful pathway to healing that affects everything from your mood and energy to your ability to recover from illness and trauma.

Dr. Sunny explains how this connection plays a crucial role in treating her patients' chronic conditions, but the implications extend far beyond serious illness. For those of us managing the daily stresses of leadership, motherhood, and entrepreneurship, understanding how our gut health affects our mental clarity, emotional resilience, and physical energy becomes essential information.

She also dives into her work supporting concussion recovery, sharing insights that go beyond traditional approaches to brain healing. Her holistic methodology addresses both the physical and neurological aspects of recovery, offering hope to families dealing with traumatic brain injuries and their long-lasting effects.

Small Changes, Massive Impact

Perhaps the most actionable part of our conversation focuses on the small changes that create big health impacts. As busy women, we often think we need to overhaul our entire lives to see meaningful improvements in our health and energy. Dr. Sunny challenges this all-or-nothing thinking with practical, sustainable approaches that fit into real life.

These aren't complicated protocols or expensive supplements—they're thoughtful adjustments to how you think about nourishing your body and supporting your nervous system. The key is consistency over intensity, and Dr. Sunny's approach makes lasting change feel achievable rather than overwhelming.

The Courage to Treat the Untreatable

What I admire most about Dr. Sunny's work is her willingness to take on cases that other doctors won't touch. This requires not just medical expertise but enormous courage and compassion. She's built her practice around serving people who have been dismissed, misunderstood, or told their symptoms are "all in their head."

There's a powerful parallel here for those of us in leadership positions. Sometimes making a real impact means being willing to tackle the problems others avoid, to serve the people others overlook, and to persist when conventional wisdom says something can't be done. Dr. Sunny's medical practice is a masterclass in this kind of courageous leadership.

Permission to Lead Differently

This conversation offers something we all need more of: permission to lead differently. Dr. Sunny's story proves that you don't have to choose between being an exceptional professional and a devoted mother. You don't have to live in the same city as your business to run it effectively. And you don't have to follow traditional models of success to create meaningful impact.

Her approach to both medicine and motherhood is deeply personal and intentionally unconventional. She's created a life and career that serves her values rather than conforming to external expectations. For those of us navigating similar tensions between our professional missions and personal priorities, her example offers both inspiration and practical wisdom.

Whether you're dealing with your own health challenges, supporting a family member through chronic illness, or simply trying to optimize your energy for the demands of leadership and motherhood, this conversation offers insights that could shift your entire approach to wellness and life design.

guestLinks.label: Learn more about Dr. Sunny’s work
Buy The Manage Her on Amazon

quiz.title: What's Your Balancing Act Style?
quiz.description: Discover how you naturally navigate the pull between your professional mission and personal priorities. Based on insights from Dr. Sunny Raleigh's approach to revolutionary medicine and motherhood.

questions.question: When you're facing a major decision about your career or family priorities, what feels most true for you?

options.text: I focus on where I can make the biggest impact with my limited time and energy
I think about who might be left without support if I don't step up
I look for the option that I can sustain long-term without burning out
I get creative and look for ways to customize a solution that fits my unique situation

questions.question: Your ideal approach to creating change in your work or community looks like:

options.text: Identifying the most critical moments to intervene and going all-in during those times
Advocating for the people who don't have a voice and filling gaps others ignore
Making small, consistent improvements that build momentum over time
Building flexible systems that can evolve and adapt as needs change

questions.question: When people describe your leadership style, they most often say:

options.text: You know exactly when to be hands-on and when to step back
You fight for the underdogs and see potential others miss
You create lasting change through patience and persistence
You find solutions that work for everyone's different circumstances

questions.question: The biggest challenge you face in balancing your personal and professional life is:

options.text: Choosing which opportunities deserve your full attention when everything feels important
Setting boundaries when you know people genuinely need your help
Sticking to your sustainable pace when others push for faster results
Getting others to understand why you need to do things differently than the standard way

questions.question: Your approach to health and wellness (for yourself or others) tends to be:

options.text: Strategic - focusing intensely on the areas that will create the most significant impact
Holistic - addressing the root causes others might overlook or dismiss
Gradual - making sustainable changes that you can maintain over time
Personalized - finding what works for your specific circumstances and needs

questions.question: When you think about your legacy or the impact you want to have, what matters most?

options.text: Being remembered for showing up fully in the moments that truly mattered
Knowing you made a difference for people who had nowhere else to turn
Creating lasting positive change through consistent, thoughtful action
Proving that there's more than one way to build a meaningful life and career

results.title: The Strategic Presence
results.description: You understand that being everywhere isn't the same as being present everywhere. Like Dr. Sunny, you've learned that quality often trumps quantity when it comes to showing up for what matters most. You make intentional choices about where to focus your energy, even when it means saying no to good opportunities. This episode will inspire you with practical strategies for distance leadership and help you refine your approach to being fully present in the moments that count.

results.title: The Compassionate Revolutionary
results.description: You're driven by a deep desire to help others, especially those who've been overlooked or underserved. You see gaps in traditional systems and feel called to fill them, even when it's challenging or unconventional. Your empathy is both your superpower and sometimes your burden. Dr. Sunny's journey of treating patients that traditional medicine gives up on will resonate deeply with you and offer insights on maintaining your mission while protecting your own well-being.

results.title: The Steady Innovator
results.description: You believe in the power of small, consistent changes to create lasting impact. Rather than dramatic overhauls, you trust in sustainable progress and incremental improvements. You're the person others come to for reliable, thoughtful solutions that actually stick. This episode will validate your approach while showing you how Dr. Sunny applies this same philosophy to both healing patients and building a practice across multiple states.

results.title: The Flexible Architect
results.description: You excel at building systems that can adapt to life's changing demands. You understand that rigid structures often break under pressure, so you create frameworks that bend without breaking. Your strength lies in finding creative solutions when traditional approaches don't fit your unique circumstances. Dr. Sunny's story of managing a medical practice from Idaho while raising teenagers will give you fresh ideas for designing a life and career that works for your specific situation.
